welcome_title: NAS Cache Setup for Steam Deck
welcome_body:
This installer will configure your Steam Deck to:

  • Mount your NAS via NFS automatically
  • Cache files you access locally
  • Keep recently played games available offline

Everything installed stays in your home folder
and survives SteamOS updates.

Make sure your NAS is reachable before continuing.
nas_roms_folder_hint: Leave blank to use root of share
cache_age_hint: How long unused files stay cached (e.g. 720 = 30 days)
cache_dir_hint: Where cached files are stored (must be in /home)
cache_mount_hint: Where your emulators should look for ROMs
cache_info:
ℹ  Point your emulators (RetroArch, RetroDeck, ES-DE)
to the mount point above after installation.
done_body:
Your NAS cache is now configured.

What to do next:

  1. Point your emulators to:
     {mount_point}

  2. Play games while at home — they get cached automatically

  3. When away, already-cached games work offline

After a SteamOS update, run this installer again
or run the repair script to recreate /var/mnt paths.
done_repair_body: Run this command to restore mount points after an update:
error_ip: Please enter a valid IP address.
error_cache_size: Cache size must be a number followed by G or M (e.g. 1G).
error_cache_age: Cache age must be a number in hours.
error_rclone_download: Failed to download rclone. Check your internet connection.
error_sudo: This step requires sudo. Enter your deck password when prompted in the terminal.
detect_status_fresh: Not installed. Run the full installer.
detect_status_repair: Partial install detected — possibly after a SteamOS update.
